revision.comment: Text replacement - "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA ==" to "== AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata--> {{Infobox religious building|image=File:阿伎留神社 正面.JPG|prefecture={{ill|Akiruno|qid=Q240480|1=Akiruno, Tokyo}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.725333|N|139.222731|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|deity={{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|qid=Q506610|1=Ajisukitakahikone}}; {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}; {{ill|Takehiratori|qid=Q11065428|1=Takehi-Nateru}}; {{ill|Ōmononushi|qid=Q10936701|1=Ōmononushi}}|architecture_style={{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}}}}
'''Akiru Shrine''' (阿伎留神社, Akiru Jinja) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|Akiruno, Tokyo|en|Akiruno, Tokyo|qid=Q240480|lt=Akiruno, Tokyo|1=Akiruno, Tokyo}}, Tokyo. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} in the {{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|qid=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}} and held the status of a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the pre-war Modern Shrine Ranking System. == Overview == Akiru Shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} as the foremost of the eight shrines in {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|qid=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}}, Tamagawa District. The shrine's name has been written as "阿伎留," "阿伎瑠," "秋留," and "畔切," all pronounced "Akiru." One theory suggests that "Akiru" derives from "畔切" (furrows), referring to the area's early agricultural development and the deity worshipped during its clearing. The shrine’s priestly lineage is said to have spanned over 70 generations, with its origins attributed to {{ill|Haji no Ren Oshio|ja|土師連男塩|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji no Ren Oshio|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, who established the shrine as a guardian deity. In the third year of {{ill|Tengyō|en|Tengyō|qid=Q1327340|lt=Tengyō|1=Tengyō}} (940 CE), {{ill|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|ja|Fujiwara no Hidesato|1=Fujiwara no Hidesato|2=ja|3=藤原秀郷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fujiwara no Hidesato|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1136623}}, a {{ill|Chinjufu_shōgun|en|Chinjufu_shōgun|qid=Q2700052|lt=Chinjufu shōgun|1=Chinjufu shōgun}}, enshrined deities from Ōharano Shrine in Kyoto, leading to the shrine being referred to as "Kasuga Daimyōjin" in the medieval period. It was also known as "Matsubara Daimyōjin" due to its location and is colloquially referred to as "Matsubara-sama." == Enshrined Deities == The shrine enshrines the following four kami: * {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}} (Ōmononushi no Kami) * {{ill|Ajisukitakahikone|ja|味耜高彦根神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ajisukitakahikone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アヂスキタカヒコネ}} (Ajisukitakahikone no Kami) * {{ill|Takehi-Nateru|ja|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|1=Takehi-Nateru|zh|建比良鳥命|1=Takehi-Nateru|fr|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|13=User:Immanuelle/Takehi-Nateru|qq=}} (Takehina no Tori no Kami) * {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋根命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天児屋命}} (Ame no Koyane no Mikoto) {{ill|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|ja|新編武蔵風土記稿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11503943}} lists only Ajisukitakahikone as the enshrined deity, suggesting it as the shrine's original principal deity.<ref name="chimei">"Tokyo no Chimei," Akiru Shrine section.</ref> == History == === Historical Background === Situated on a terrace overlooking the {{ill|Aki_River|en|Aki_River|qid=Q23043420|lt=Aki River|1=Aki River}}, the shrine likely originated as a place of worship for the local community during the area's early agricultural development.<ref name="chimei"/> According to the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|en|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qid=Q1052981|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}}, the shrine was granted a promotion to Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade, on July 15, 884 CE. Subsequent offerings and promotions were made over time. The shrine is listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} (927 CE) as "Akiru Shrine" in Tama District, Musashi Province. In the {{ill|Kamakura period|en|Kamakura period|qid=Q236205|lt=Kamakura period|1=Kamakura period}}, the shrine was revered by military leaders such as {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, {{ill|Ashikaga Takauji|en|Ashikaga Takauji|qid=Q297107|lt=Ashikaga Takauji|1=Ashikaga Takauji}}, and the Hōjō clan, who donated shrine lands. During the Edo period, {{ill|Tokugawa Ieyasu|en|Tokugawa Ieyasu|qid=Q171977|lt=Tokugawa Ieyasu|1=Tokugawa Ieyasu}} granted 10 koku of land to the shrine in 1591, a practice continued by successive shōguns. === Modern Era === In 1873, Akiru Shrine was designated as a {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} under the Modern Shrine Ranking System. Later, in 1907, it was designated as a shrine eligible to receive government offerings. It became a registered religious corporation in 1954. === Shrine Buildings === The original shrine buildings were destroyed by fire during the Tenpō era (1830–1844). Reconstruction was delayed due to political upheaval, and the current main hall and worship hall were completed in 1888. Further renovations and additions, including a grand torii gate and a kagura hall, were made throughout the 20th century. The shrine also maintains an {{ill|Otabisho|en|Otabisho|qid=Q11489117|lt=Otabisho|1=Otabisho}} in central Akiruno ({{coord|35|43|42.67|N|139|13|23.16|E|region:JP-13|name=Otabisho|qq=}}). <gallery mode="packed" widths="150px" heights="193px"> File:阿伎留神社 本殿.JPG|Main hall (from rear-left) Ahiruno-jinja(Ahiruno) Kaguraden.jpg|Kagura hall Akiru-jinja(Ahiruno) Mikoshiden.jpg|Portable shrine storage </gallery> == Festivals and Events == === Annual Festivals === [[File:Akiru Shrine Taisai.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Annual Grand Festival]] [[File:Akiru Shrine Mikoshi.jpg|thumb|250px|right|Hexagonal Mikoshi]] * January 1: New Year's Festival * February 3: Setsubun Festival * February 22: Kinensai (Harvest Festival) * September 28–30: Grand Festival ("Itsukaichi Festival") * November 15: Shichigosan Festival * December 31: Ōharae (Great Purification) === Grand Festival (Itsukaichi Festival) === The annual Grand Festival held from September 28 to 30 features a rare hexagonal mikoshi (portable shrine) weighing approximately 600 kilograms, which leads a procession through the streets. This is followed by smaller mikoshi from various neighborhoods. The festival also includes traditional dances, such as the Itsukaichi lion dance, and numerous food and game stalls. == Cultural Properties == === Akiruno City Designated Cultural Properties === * ''Twelve Annual Festival Rituals'' (絵巻) ''' A vividly colored scroll by Kano Keiun depicting the shrine's monthly rituals from the late Edo period. Designated in 1969. * Hanging Buddhist image plate (工芸品) ''' Inscribed with "Main deity of Matsubara Daimyōjin" and dated to 1338. Designated in 1969. * ''Documents of the Bushū Minami Ikki'' (古文書) ''' Six historical documents related to the medieval local warrior groups, designated in 1969. == Access == * Address: 1081 Itsukaichi, Akiruno-shi, Tokyo * By Train: Approximately 15 minutes' walk from {{ill|Musashi-Itsukaichi Station|en|Musashi-Itsukaichi Station|qid=Q6940131|lt=Musashi-Itsukaichi Station|1=Musashi-Itsukaichi Station}} on the {{ill|Itsukaichi Line|en|Itsukaichi Line|qid=Q1323839|lt=JR Itsukaichi Line|1=Itsukaichi Line}}. == References == {{reflist|qq=}}
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo|ja|東京都の神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Shinto shrines in Tokyo|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|Musashi Province|en|Musashi Province|qid=Q907398|lt=Musashi Province|1=Musashi Province}} == External Links == * {{ill|Shinto Jinja Source Compilation|ja|神道・神社史料集成|surl=http://www.tokyo-jinjacho.or.jp/nishitama/akiruno/5491/|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto Jinja Source Compilation|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * [http://i-kamicho.com/akiru.html Akiru Shrine Official Site]
{{translated page|ja|阿伎留神社|version=101576757|comment=Imported full ja history}}
<!--Akiru Shrine--><!--Akiru Shrine--><!--Akiru Shrine--><!--Akiru Shrine--><!--Akiru Shrine--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Akiru-jinja}} == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]]
'''Akiru Shrine (Q11657213)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Musashi Province]].
== Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Akiru Shrine|lt=Akiru Shrine|ja|阿伎留神社|commons|Category:Akiru-jinja|qid=Q11657213|1=Akiru Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|35.725348|139.222604|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181692 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q11657213}} [[da:d:Q11657213]] [[ja:阿伎留神社]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages with duplicated content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]]==Notes== <references group="lower-alpha"/> [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Junior Fourth Rank]] [[Category:Senior Fifth Rank]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Akiruno, Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Akiruno]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokyo]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokyo]] [[Category:Municipal Tangible Cultural Properties in Tokyo Wards]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]]
